claim_bullets.bullet_text: In JavaScript, arrays and objects can mutate while maintaining identity, as shown by modifying array elements or adding new properties to objects (raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.141-143).
Reassignment of a binding creates a new value, while mutation modifies the existing value that multiple bindings can reference (raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.141-143).
The `const` keyword prevents rebinding but not mutation, which is an important distinction in understanding JavaScript's semantics (raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.141-143).

source_record_text: Chapter on mutation in JavaScriptAllonge, covering arrays, objects, reassignment, mutation, and the const keyword's limitations.
